domain:
In an automated facility, a fleet of robots is deployed, where each one is recognized by a unique identifier. These robots perform tasks based on their function, some are cleaning robots, while others serve as transport robots. In the facility, the robots are assigned to a set of tasks, each distinguished by a unique code. Throughout the operation, a robot may be assigned to perform various tasks. 

When a robot performs a task, the execution  is identified by a unique code and occurs at a specific starting time. Finally, each robot is assigned to a location within the facility, which can be either a corridor or a room.

questions.question:
Should id be modeled as a separate class associated with Facility, or remain as a simple representation?

For example:
1. Id is a separate class with its own attributes. This allows users to query and filter by individual id properties.
2. Id is modeled as a simple representation in Facility. This is suitable when id details are not needed.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The Id is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The id is a simple representation in Facility.
questions.selected: Option 2: The id is a simple representation in Facility.

questions.question:
Should the id in facility be modeled as a an attribute which allows free text input, or as an enumeration with predefined literals?

For example:
1. Use id as string attribute in facility, which allows any text value.
2. Use id as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: robot1, robot2, robot3, robot4 or robot5.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for id.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Enumeration id with predefined values: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for id.

questions.question:
Should the relationship between Facility and Robot be modeled as a composition or as an association?

For example:
1. Use composition, Robot would depend on Facility for its existence.
2. Use association, Robot could exist independently of Facility, and can also be linked to multiple facilities.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Robot depends on Facility.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Robot exists independently of Facility.
questions.selected: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Robot exists independently of Facility.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Facility in the relationship with Robot?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a robot can exist without any associated facility.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a robot must have at least one associated facility.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Robot can exist without Facility.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Robot must have at least one Facility.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Robot can exist without Facility.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Robot in the relationship with Facility?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a facility can exist without any associated robot.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a facility must have at least one associated robot.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Facility can exist without Robot.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Facility must have at least one Robot.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Facility can exist without Robot.

questions.question:

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a task can exist without any associated robot.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a task must have at least one associated robot.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Task can exist without Robot.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Task must have at least one Robot.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Task can exist without Robot.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of Robot in the relationship with Task?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a task is associated with exactly one robot.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a task can be associated with multiple robots.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each task links to one robot.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each task can link to multiple robots.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each task can link to multiple robots.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 2: Many as maximum cardinality, each task can link to multiple robots.

This option is better because the domain description states that a robot may be assigned to perform various tasks, implying that multiple robots can be assigned to the same task.

questions.question:
Should cleaning robot and transport robot be represented as separate subclasses of Robot, or as instances of Robot distinguished by an enumeration attribute?
Consider whether these types have distinct attributes or behavior.

For example:
1. CleaningRobot and TransportRobot are modeled as separate subclasses of Robot. Each subclass can have its own specific attributes and methods.
2. A single Robot class with a Type enumeration attribute with literals: cleaning robot or transport robot. All types share the same structure.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Use inheritance, create separate subclasses for cleaning robot and transport robot.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Use enumeration Robot type with with literals: cleaning robot or transport robot.
questions.selected: Option 2: Use enumeration Robot type with with literals: cleaning robot or transport robot.
